name: Davis Minimal Media (Sigma) + glucose (PMC7266357)
description: Davis Minimal Media (Sigma) + glucose — medium reported in PMC7266357 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC7266357 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.base_expansion: Base 'Davis' expanded to standard composition: Davis minimal medium (Davis & Mingioli 1950): K2HPO4/KH2PO4, (NH4)2SO4, Na-citrate, MgSO4 + glucose.
provenance.verification_evidence: Overnight cultures of resistant and sensitive cells for each experiment were grown from single colonies and then incubated in sterile Davis Minimal Media with 1,000 g/ml glucose liquid media overnight at 30 C while rotating at 240 rpm. (the '1,000 g/ml' is a typographical error for 1,000 ug/ml = 1 g/L; agar plates used 2,000 ug/ml). The Sigma Davis Minimal Broth base also supplies dextrose at 1.0 g/L.
provenance.base_medium: Davis Minimal Broth (Davis formulation; Davis & Mingioli 1950), Sigma-Aldrich product 93753 / Difco-BD 275610 / HiMedia M389

sources_chased.what: Full-text methods (PMC7266357, PLoS Biol) to identify the medium and its glucose supplement
Commercial Davis Minimal Broth formula (paper cites only 'Sigma' with no literature reference)
sources_chased.how: WebSearch of Sigma-Aldrich 93753 / Davis Minimal Agar 79332 datasheets, BD 275610, and HiMedia M389 datasheet (Sigma/HiMedia PDFs timed out; formula recovered verbatim from datasheet text via search)

recuration.notes: Davis Minimal Broth is a fully defined minimal medium of six salts plus dextrose; it contains NO added trace-element mix and NO vitamins (E. coli is prototrophic and grows on it), so no hidden stock solution needs expanding and nothing was genuinely missing. The target's current_components already correctly decompose the salts into ions (K, Pi, NH4, SO4, citrate, Mg, Na) plus glucose/O2/H2O/H+; this record adds the compound-level recipe with concentrations. Paper is not anaerobic despite 'facultative' organism label: cultures are shaken (240 rpm) and stirred bioreactors, i.e. aerobic. Glucose
